summary: Large item.c cleanup and matching pass that de-externed several item .bss globals, removed the matching asm slice for code_80266F3C.s, simplified many item routines, tightened prototypes and types, and moved item allocation/counter state toward named C structs instead of anonymous aggregate access. Review focused on preserving matching-sensitive idioms, using clearer decomp types such as BOOL, ITKIND, NULL, and any_t, and documenting functions with Doxygen-style brief comments.

key_files.role: Main rework: item functions rewritten for cleaner matches, .bss globals defined in C, boolean/prototype cleanup, helper inlines, pointer/null cleanup, item counter and spawn logic simplification.
Header adjusted for item structs/types and declarations used by the reworked item.c; exact details are only partially visible in the provided slice.
Removed .bss definitions now emitted from C, reducing assembly reliance for item.c data.
Added static inline HSD_GObjGetHSDObj to pair with existing HSD_GObjGetUserData and make GObj access clearer/more matchable.
Updated GObj_InitUserData callback prototype to use any_t after review pushed back on phasing any_t out too early.
Minor one-line adjustment related to item changes; exact content not visible in the excerpt.

what_changed: Converted func_80266F3C from s32 returning 0/1 to BOOL returning FALSE/TRUE, and reused it in func_80266F70 instead of recomputing the item-enabled check.
Changed literal boolean-like calls such as func_8027870C(0) to func_8027870C(FALSE).

De-externed item .bss data: labels such as lbl_804A0BE0, lbl_804A0C0C, lbl_804A0C38, lbl_804A0C64, lbl_804A0CCC, and lbl_804A0E24 are now defined in src/melee/it/item.c, while their old assembly .skip declarations were removed from asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s.

Reworked func_80266FCC item initialization to call HSD_ObjAllocInit with sizeof(Item), sizeof(DynamicBoneTable), and sizeof(ItemLink), then initialize named global structs directly instead of accessing a larger opaque HSD_ObjAllocUnkStruct wrapper.
Introduced helper inlines such as HSD_JObjSetFacingDirItem and ItUnkHoldKind to factor small repeated/awkward item setup patterns while preserving intent.
Added HSD_GObjGetHSDObj inline accessor in gobj.h and used HSD_GObjGetUserData/HSD_GObjGetHSDObj casts in item setup code.

Simplified many functions by removing redundant temporaries, gotos, casts, and explicit 0U comparisons; examples include item coordinate initialization, camera-box removal, hold-kind count increment/decrement, drop-item checks, and render/JObj setup.
Replaced integer/pointer ambiguity with NULL for pointer fields such as x518_ownerGObj, x520_cameraBox, and reviewed xBBC_dynamicBoneTable.
Used domain-specific enum-like types in reviewed spots, especially ITKIND for item id/index variables and GA_Air for ground/air state comparisons.

Reworked item article/logic table pointer selection in func_80267978 into direct category branches for common items, character items, Pokemon, and stage items, using calculated indices into lbl_804D6D24, lbl_804D6D38, lbl_804D6D30, lbl_804A0F60 and corresponding logic tables.
Changed GObj_InitUserData remove_func prototype to void (*remove_func)(any_t), retaining any_t per review rather than switching directly to void*.
Replaced older inline comments containing addresses/decomp.me scratch links with shorter Doxygen-style /// descriptions for several functions.
smart_moves: De-externing .bss labels into C made item.c more self-contained and allowed the assembly file to shrink without changing the underlying storage layout.
Using sizeof(Item), sizeof(DynamicBoneTable), and sizeof(ItemLink) in allocation initialization captured struct knowledge and reduced magic constants such as 0xFCC, 0x190, and 0x1D8.

Directly naming the split global allocation/counter structures, for example lbl_804A0C64 and lbl_804A0CCC, avoided awkward nested accesses through an opaque aggregate and clarified which state each function mutates.
Booleanizing item-enabled/drop-item helpers made call sites clearer and enabled reuse of func_80266F3C in func_80266F70.
The HSD_GObjGetHSDObj inline mirrors HSD_GObjGetUserData and documents GObj field access without forcing every item routine to touch struct fields directly.
Factoring the facing-direction rotation and hold-kind damage-table update into small static inline helpers reduced noisy temps while keeping matching-sensitive code local to item.c.
Review preserved direct array indexing for article and item logic table selection because the reviewer noted that this matches HAL's style, avoiding over-abstraction that could harm matches.
decomp_lessons: When moving .bss out of asm, define the exact C objects in the owning source file and remove only the corresponding .skip labels from assembly.
Prefer domain types like BOOL, ITKIND, and enum constants such as GA_Air when evidence supports them; this improves readability and often still matches.
Use NULL for pointer fields and FALSE/TRUE for booleans instead of 0, 0U, or 1 when the field/function semantics are known.
Allocation initialization should use sizeof(named_struct) once struct identity is known; it records layout understanding and reduces future constant drift.
Do not hide HAL-style direct array access behind unnecessary abstractions if the original pattern is simple indexed table lookup.
Small static inline helpers can improve readability in matching code, but keep them narrow and evidence-based so they do not obscure control flow.
For matching-only stack padding, guard unused arrays under MUST_MATCH rather than leaving unexplained filler in normal builds.
When a type migration like any_t to void* is not settled project-wide, follow the existing convention until there is a clear phasing procedure.
Function comments should be brief Doxygen-style descriptions or header docs; avoid preserving decomp.me scratch links and noisy address comments as primary documentation.
If a function is still messy but matching, mark it with a TODO instead of pretending the decomp is semantically polished.
naming_conventions: Use BOOL return type with TRUE/FALSE for predicate-style functions such as func_80266F3C and func_8026784C.
Use ITKIND for item IDs and item-kind-derived indices when dealing with spawnItem->x8_item_id or item_data->x10_item_kind.
Use NULL for GObj, camera box, dynamic bone table, and other pointer fields.
Use Doxygen-style triple-slash comments such as /// Check if items are enabled, /// Remove Camera Box, and /// Store Item Pointers.
Prefer descriptive local names like item_data, spawnItem, item_gobj, jobj, idx, and result over compiler-like temporaries when they still match.
Keep legacy global labels such as lbl_804A0C64 when no better symbol name is established, but improve their field/struct typing.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed assembly .bss labels for item allocation globals after defining equivalent C globals with matching types and alignment expectations.
Replaced magic allocation sizes with sizeof calls, which can still match if the struct sizes are correct.
Simplified goto-heavy switch logic into a BOOL result variable plus break/return pattern for func_8026784C.
Used post-increment directly in func_802676F4 for func_80274EE8(lbl_804A0C64.x10++), preserving the old-use-then-increment behavior while reducing temps.
Used static inline wrappers for small codegen-sensitive operations such as GObj field access and item facing rotation.
Reviewer suggested wrapping artificial stack padding as #ifdef MUST_MATCH u8 unused[8];, showing a preferred pattern for unavoidable filler.
Preserved direct table indexing for item article data and logic tables because reviewer said that is what HAL does.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Function comments should ideally move to headers, but if kept in source they should at least be brief Doxygen-style comments, e.g. /// Check to load ItCo.dat/usd.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Keep any_t in GObj_InitUserData callback signatures for now until the project has a procedure for phasing it out.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Use ITKIND for item id/index variables, e.g. ITKIND ItemID = spawnItem->x8_item_id and ITKIND idx = spawnItem->x8_item_id - It_Kind_Kuriboh.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Direct array accesses in item table selection are acceptable because that is what HAL does.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: For matching-only unused stack space, prefer #ifdef MUST_MATCH u8 unused[8];.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Mark func_802682F0 with a TODO/brief noting it needs serious cleaning while retaining BOOL signature.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Use NULL for pointer fields such as xBBC_dynamicBoneTable instead of integer 0.

follow_up_queries: Inspect src/melee/it/item.h in PR 667 for the exact struct/type changes that enabled sizeof(Item), DynamicBoneTable, ItemLink, and S32Vec3.
Search later PRs for any_t phase-out decisions after the GObj_InitUserData review comment.
Find subsequent cleanups of func_802682F0, especially whether the TODO about serious cleaning was resolved.
Check whether lbl_804A0C64, lbl_804A0CCC, and related item globals later received semantic names.
Search for later uses of HSD_GObjGetHSDObj to see whether the inline became a project-wide convention.
